AgentAction.description: Generic Agent Inbox envelope for calendar-api ActionResponse rows.
AgentAction.title: AgentAction

AgentActionStatus.description: BFF-owned status for generic agent actions.
AgentActionStatus.title: AgentActionStatus

ChannelPrefs.description:
Coaching-delivery prefs for a messaging channel. Delivery isn't wired yet
(see MessagingChannelList.deliveryLive) — these persist intent only.
ChannelPrefs.title: ChannelPrefs

CompletedSession.description: BFF-owned mirror of the draft CompletedSession schema.
CompletedSession.title: CompletedSession

CompletionsListResponse.description:
Response for GET /v1/workouts/completions.

Mirrors mapper-api's list_workout_completions_endpoint return shape:
{success, completions: [...], total}.
CompletionsListResponse.title: CompletionsListResponse

Conflict.description: BFF-owned iOS Conflict shape for GET /v1/planning/conflicts.
Conflict.title: Conflict

ConflictSeverity.description: BFF-owned Conflict.severity enum from the coach-loop draft contract.
ConflictSeverity.title: ConflictSeverity

ConflictType.description: BFF-owned Conflict.type enum from the coach-loop draft contract.
ConflictType.title: ConflictType

ConnectedCalendarSummary.description:
One connected third-party feed for the iOS Connected-apps screen.

DELIBERATELY omits `ics_url`: the feed URL carries a subscription token and
must never reach the client (same token-safety as the W-A/W-B host-only
logging). The screen needs identity + sync health, not the raw feed URL.
`lastSync`/`syncStatus`/`syncErrorMessage` back the "last synced <time>" /
error caption so a stale feed surfaces honestly (AMA-2000 §1B), never a
silent empty row.
ConnectedCalendarSummary.title: ConnectedCalendarSummary

DayState.description: BFF-owned mirror of the draft DayState schema (camelCase on the wire).
DayState.title: DayState

DegradedResponse.description: Returned with HTTP 503 when a capability is unroutable. iOS shows user_message.
DegradedResponse.title: DegradedResponse

DeviceRole.description:
Which metric a device feeds (PairingScreen role chips). Multi-per-device
AND multi-device-per-role; conflicts resolved by most-recently-synced wins
(screens-flow.jsx:360). Assigned in Wedge C (AMA-2030).
DeviceRole.title: DeviceRole

DeviceRolesRequest.description:
Body for PUT /v1/devices/{id}/roles (AMA-2030, Wedge C).

Typed against the DeviceRole enum, so an unknown role is rejected at the BFF
(422) before it ever reaches mapper-api / the device_roles CHECK constraint.
DeviceRolesRequest.title: DeviceRolesRequest

DeviceRolesResult.description: Response for PUT /v1/devices/{id}/roles — the device's roles after the write.
DeviceRolesResult.title: DeviceRolesResult

EquipmentInventory.description:
Structured equipment inventory (camelCase wire shape: dumbbellRangeKg,
trainingLocation). Single set per user.
EquipmentInventory.title: EquipmentInventory

ErrorCode.description: All typed error codes returned by AmakaFlow services. Single source of truth.
ErrorCode.title: ErrorCode

ErrorEnvelope.description: Stable BFF error envelope for planning upstream failures.
ErrorEnvelope.title: ErrorEnvelope

ExerciseFollowAlongStep.description: One exercise step (with reps / weight / station / media).
ExerciseFollowAlongStep.title: ExerciseFollowAlongStep

GenerateWeekRequest.description: BFF-owned request body for weekly plan proposal generation.
GenerateWeekRequest.title: GenerateWeekRequest

GoalEntry.description:
One training goal (AMA-2018, D5 W1). camelCase wire shape (strengthSubtype).
Multi-select; `none` is mutually exclusive (validated upstream in chat-api).
GoalEntry.title: GoalEntry

HealthMetrics.description: Health metrics captured during the workout (Apple Watch / Garmin).
HealthMetrics.title: HealthMetrics

LibraryItem.description:
One saved-content card for the Library, projected from a knowledge_card.

`thumbnailUrl`/`bookmarked` are not captured yet (no OG-image/bookmark on
knowledge_cards) — null/false until that ingest+schema work lands (see the
Library follow-up). No fabricated values.
LibraryItem.title: LibraryItem

LibraryItemDetail.description:
Response for GET /v1/library/items/{id} — LibraryItem + the body we have.

No `raw_content`/structured workout/plan/notes from knowledge_cards yet, so the
body is `summary` + `keyTakeaways`; structured bodies + notes are deferred
(AMA-2046 / v1.1). No fabricated content.
LibraryItemDetail.title: LibraryItemDetail

LibraryKind.description:
Content kind for the Library filter chips. Derived from the card's
source_type (no stored `kind` column needed).
LibraryKind.title: LibraryKind

MessagingChannel.description: One messaging provider for the MessagingScreen.
MessagingChannel.title: MessagingChannel

PairDeviceRequest.description:
Request body for POST /v1/devices/pair (AMA-2029, Wedge B).

The user reads the 6-digit code shown on their Garmin watch (the CIQ widget
obtained it via /api/garmin/pair/request) and enters it here. The BFF
forwards it (snake_case `short_code`) to mapper-api /api/garmin/pair/confirm
with the caller's Clerk JWT, which links the watch to the account.
PairDeviceRequest.title: PairDeviceRequest

PairDeviceResult.description:
Response for POST /v1/devices/pair and DELETE /v1/devices/{id}.

`success`/`message` are single words, so camelCase == the snake_case mapper
returns — the upstream body validates as-is.
PairDeviceResult.title: PairDeviceResult

PairedDevice.description:
One paired device for PairingScreen. Typed projection over mapper-api
GET /mobile/pairing/devices (untyped dict upstream): flattens device_info,
surfaces the stable device_info.device_id as `id`, and maps paired_at →
lastSyncAt (Wedge A proxy; real last-sync is AMA-2032).
PairedDevice.title: PairedDevice

ParseTextRequest.description: BFF-owned request body for freeform workout text parsing.
ParseTextRequest.title: ParseTextRequest

ParseTextResult.description: Parsed exercise list returned by POST /v1/ingest/parse-text.
ParseTextResult.title: ParseTextResult

ParsedExercise.description: Faithful mobile-facing mirror of ingestor ParsedExerciseResponse.
ParsedExercise.title: ParsedExercise

PendingSyncWorkout.description:
One pending workout entry returned by /sync/pending.

Intervals stay as a free-form list of dicts — the upstream router
runs `to_workoutkit(...).intervals` and the resulting WorkoutKit
interval shapes are large and themselves polymorphic (warmup,
cooldown, repeat, single). Modeling them strictly in v1 would either
drift from upstream or pull a large dependency tree into the BFF;
deferring per the AMA-1817 1:1-mirror principle.
PendingSyncWorkout.title: PendingSyncWorkout

PlannedSession.description: BFF-owned mirror of the draft PlannedSession schema.
PlannedSession.title: PlannedSession

PlannedWorkout.description:
Subset of `workout_events` returned to iOS.

Aliases (camelCase) preserved from mapper-api so the wire shape stays
identical across BFF and upstream — iOS already decodes this format.
PlannedWorkout.title: PlannedWorkout

ProposedDay.description: One day of a proposed week, including synthesized rest days.
ProposedDay.title: ProposedDay

ReadinessSourcePref.description: One per-metric source choice (AMA-2053 Wedge D). Wire shape: metric, source, deviceId.
ReadinessSourcePref.title: ReadinessSourcePref

ReadinessSourcePrefs.description: A user's per-metric source preferences (AMA-2053 Wedge D).
ReadinessSourcePrefs.title: ReadinessSourcePrefs

ReadinessToday.description:
Today's raw recovery metrics for the iOS readiness sheet (AMA-2050).

Every metric is nullable — honest-null when absent. `hasData` is False when
there's no daily_status row today (honest "no data yet", never a fabricated
zero). No composite readiness *score* — that's AMA-1565.
ReadinessToday.title: ReadinessToday

ReadinessTrend.description:
Passive N-day trend for one readiness metric (AMA-2051 Wedge B).

Points cover only days with a reading; missing days are absent (honest gaps,
never interpolated). No composite score (AMA-1565).
ReadinessTrend.title: ReadinessTrend

ReadinessTrendPoint.description: One day in a metric's trend; value null = honest gap (AMA-2051 Wedge B).
ReadinessTrendPoint.title: ReadinessTrendPoint

RiskLevel.description: BFF-owned risk level for generic agent actions.
RiskLevel.title: RiskLevel

SetEntry.description: Individual set within an exercise log (AMA-281).
SetEntry.title: SetEntry

SetLog.description: Log of sets for a single exercise (AMA-281).
SetLog.title: SetLog

SimulationConfig.description: Simulation parameters when workout runs in simulation mode (AMA-273).
SimulationConfig.title: SimulationConfig

TelegramSetupResponse.description:
Response for POST /v1/messaging/telegram/setup — a one-time deep-link token.
Typed projection over mapper-api /api/telegram/link-token (snake_case).
TelegramSetupResponse.title: TelegramSetupResponse

TelegramStatusResponse.description: Response for GET /v1/messaging/telegram/status — has the bot consumed the token.
TelegramStatusResponse.title: TelegramStatusResponse

TimeBlock.description: BFF-owned mirror of the draft TimeBlock schema.
TimeBlock.title: TimeBlock

WatchDeliveryState.description: The 5 PRODUCT-SPEC §4.8 delivery states (mirrors mapper DeliveryState).
WatchDeliveryState.title: WatchDeliveryState

WatchDeliveryStatus.description:
Response for GET /v1/devices/watch-delivery/{workout_id}. iOS derives the
emoji/tone from `state`; `title`/`subtitle` are the human labels.
WatchDeliveryStatus.title: WatchDeliveryStatus

WorkoutCompletionItem.description:
One row from the user's completion history.

The upstream router returns whatever the CompletionRepository's
get_user_completions() emits — a loosely-shaped dict that includes
health metrics, workout linkage IDs, fidelity, summary fields, etc.
Per the AMA-1817 1:1-mirror principle (and matching how PendingSyncWorkout
handles upstream polymorphism), we leave non-load-bearing fields
open via model_config extra="allow" rather than narrowing in v1.
WorkoutCompletionItem.title: WorkoutCompletionItem

WorkoutCompletionRequest.description:
Request body for POST /v1/workouts/complete.

Mirrors `WorkoutCompletionRequest` in mapper-api
(services/mapper-api/backend/workout_completions.py) 1:1.

Note: `execution_log`, `heart_rate_samples`, `workout_structure`,
`intervals`, and `device_info` stay as free-form dicts here — the
upstream model deliberately uses `Dict[str, Any]` after the AMA-1813
saga (the strict ExecutionLog tree rejected the iOS payload). We do
NOT narrow these in v1; that's reserved for a future contract ticket.

client_generated_id.description: Idempotency key — same value on every retry of the same logical completion.
client_generated_id.title: Client Generated Id

WorkoutCompletionRequest.title: WorkoutCompletionRequest

WorkoutCompletionResponse.description:
Response for POST /v1/workouts/complete.

Mirrors mapper-api's `WorkoutCompletionResponse` plus the optional
`fidelity`, `idempotent`, and `error_code` fields that the router
actually emits in practice (see record_workout_completion_endpoint).
WorkoutCompletionResponse.title: WorkoutCompletionResponse

WorkoutCompletionSummary.description: Summary echoed back after a successful completion.
WorkoutCompletionSummary.title: WorkoutCompletionSummary

BearerAuth.description: Clerk-issued JWT, forwarded verbatim to upstream services.

info.description: Mobile Backend-for-Frontend (AMA-1819 + AMA-1827). Thin proxy for the mobile-facing endpoints under /v1/. iOS calls this service exclusively for workout/sync/coach/chat/knowledge/nutrition flows so internal upstream renames don't require an iOS release.
info.title: AmakaFlow Mobile BFF

get.description: Health probe - used by Render and any load balancer in front.
iOS Agent Inbox read → calendar-api GET /actions.
get.summary: (W3a) Agent action log — generic AgentAction envelope

post.description: iOS Agent Inbox decision → calendar-api POST /actions/{id}/approve|reject.
post.summary: (W3b) Approve or reject an agent action

post.description: iOS Agent Inbox undo → calendar-api POST /actions/{id}/undo.

get.description: iOS fetchCoachMemories → chat-api GET /coach/memories.
iOS coaching/equipment profile read → chat-api GET /coach/profile.

put.description: iOS onboarding/equipment profile save → chat-api PUT /coach/profile.

get.description:
iOS Connected-apps screen → calendar-api GET /calendar/connected-calendars,
projected to the typed summary (ics_url dropped — token safety). Feeds the
"last synced" / error caption so a stale feed surfaces honestly (AMA-2000).

iOS PairingScreen device list → mapper-api GET /mobile/pairing/devices,
reshaped into the typed PairedDevice list (AMA-2026).

post.description:
iOS "Add device" → mapper-api POST /api/garmin/pair/confirm (AMA-2029).

The 6-digit code is displayed on the Garmin watch (the CIQ widget called
/pair/request); the user enters it here and we confirm with their Clerk JWT.
camelCase `shortCode` in → snake_case `short_code` upstream.

get.description: iOS WatchDeliveryScreen poll → mapper-api GET /api/workout-delivery/status (AMA-2028).

post.description:
iOS Resend → mapper-api POST /api/workout-delivery/{id}/resend (AMA-2028).
Mapper returns 202 on success; 409 (state can't resend) / 422 (no Garmin) flow through.

put.description:
iOS role chips → mapper-api PUT /mobile/pairing/devices/{id}/roles (AMA-2030).

Roles are typed against the DeviceRole enum (FastAPI 422s an unknown role
before it reaches mapper). The backend resolves {id} → device_type and
upserts device_roles keyed on (clerk_user_id, device_type).

get.description: iOS fetchXP → chat-api GET /gamification/xp.

post.summary: (W4) Parse freeform workout text into a structured exercise list

get.description: iOS KnowledgeService.listCards → chat-api GET /knowledge/cards.
iOS KnowledgeService.searchCards → chat-api GET /knowledge/cards/search.

iOS Library → chat-api GET /knowledge/cards, projected to typed LibraryItems
(kind derived from source_type, domain from source_url). `kind` filters
BFF-side (derived); `tag` passes through upstream (AMA-2004).

iOS LibraryDetail → chat-api GET /knowledge/cards/{id} (AMA-2005).

LibraryItem fields + the body we have (summary + key_takeaways). Structured
workout/plan bodies + notes are deferred (AMA-2046 / v1.1).

iOS MessagingScreen → mapper-api GET /api/messaging/channels (AMA-2027).

Reshapes the snake_case channel catalog (Telegram connection + WhatsApp/Slack
coming-soon + per-channel prefs) into the typed camelCase list. `deliveryLive`
is False until the briefing/checkin/swap senders exist.

post.description:
iOS TelegramSetup → mapper-api POST /api/telegram/link-token (AMA-2031).

Mints a one-time deep-link token. snake_case upstream → camelCase out.

get.description: iOS TelegramSetup polling → mapper-api GET /api/telegram/link-status (AMA-2031).
iOS lookupBarcode → chat-api GET /nutrition/barcode/{code}.
iOS getFuelingStatus → chat-api GET /nutrition/fueling-status.
iOS planning conflict read → calendar-api GET /days + POST /detect-conflicts.
get.summary: (W2) Detect scheduling conflicts for a date range

get.description: iOS Home/Fatigue day-state read → calendar-api GET /days.

schema.title: Response V1 Planning Days V1 Planning Days Get

post.description: iOS weekly planning proposal → calendar-api POST /generate-week.

put.description:
iOS HealthKitHRVService → chat-api PUT /coach/readiness-sample.

Writes a daily recovery sample (HRV/RHR/sleep) into daily_status with
source=apple_health. Thin passthrough — chat-api owns validation +
the daily_status upsert. Read side = /v1/readiness/today + /trend.

get.description:
iOS Readiness source-picker → chat-api GET /coach/readiness-source-prefs, typed.
Honest-empty when no prefs set (client falls back to defaults). No fabricated prefs.

put.description:
iOS Readiness source-picker write → chat-api PUT /coach/readiness-source-prefs, typed.
Upstream validation (422) + DB-down (503) are propagated with their status so the client
surfaces a real error (CTAError), not a generic failure.

get.description:
iOS readiness sheet → chat-api GET /coach/readiness-today, projected to
the typed camelCase shape. Honest-null per metric; hasData=False when no
row today (no fabricated zero). No composite score (AMA-1565).

iOS readiness sheet → chat-api GET /coach/readiness-trend, projected to the
typed camelCase shape. Passive trend; null points = honest gaps. No composite
score (AMA-1565).
SyncEngine pending-flush read → mapper-api GET /sync/pending.

post.description: Save & End on iOS → mapper-api POST /workouts/complete.

get.description:
iOS Verify / Reopen completions read (AMA-1841) -> mapper-api GET /workouts/completions.

CJ-01 Verify + Reopen depend on this route — iOS reads the user's
completion history through the BFF. Query params (limit, offset,
include_simulated) and the Authorization header are forwarded
verbatim by `_proxy`.
iOS Home / Calendar planned-workouts read → mapper-api GET /workouts/planned.

iOS FollowAlongScreen read (AMA-1917) → mapper-api GET /workouts/{id}/follow-along.

Typed via FollowAlongResponse so the OpenAPI emit carries the
discriminated-union step shape (ExerciseFollowAlongStep / RestFollowAlongStep)
per ADR-008. iOS swift-openapi-generator picks this up to produce a
typed Swift sealed/oneOf result.

The Authorization header is forwarded verbatim by `_proxy`. Upstream
typing was added in AMA-1909 / PR #433 — this BFF route exposes the
same shape to iOS via the AMA-1820 / AMA-1824 contract surface.
